What is the deadline for session reporting at JKUAT?
ground_truth: By the end of the first week of every semester.

question: What happens if a student does not session report by the deadline?
ground_truth: The student is marked absent for that semester and may face academic suspension. 3

question: Where does a student go to complete session reporting?
ground_truth: The JKUAT Student Portal at portal.jkuat.ac.ke, under Academics then Session Re- porting.

question: Is session reporting mandatory for students on attachment?
ground_truth: Yes. It is mandatory for all registered students including those on attachment or special programmes.

question: What is the unit registration deadline at JKUAT?
ground_truth: By the end of the fourth week of the semester.

question: Will a student who has not cleared household fees be allowed to sit exams?
ground_truth: No. Students who have not cleared household/hostel fees in full will not be permitted to sit any University examinations for that semester.

question: What is the minimum class attendance required to sit examinations?
What documents must a student present at the examination hall?
ground_truth: A valid Student Identity Card, unit registration card, and for on-campus students a household fee clearance slip.

question: What grade is awarded to a student denied entry to an examination?
ground_truth: A grade of IC (Incomplete) or AB (Absent), which may lead to a fail grade upon processing.

question: Where does a student obtain household fee clearance?
ground_truth: From the Hostels and Accommodation Office.

question: When is ICMPS 2026 taking place?
ground_truth: 6th and 7th July 2026 at JKUAT Main Campus, Juja.
